title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Type-System Trace: Robots Legal Boundary
summary: A request to audit a public website names pages to collect but no owner, user decision, permitted purpose, stop contact, privacy boundary, or proof that findings are useful. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Legal Boundary model · Theory Robots Lega…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Robots Legal Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Legal Boundary model · Theory Robots Lega…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 7849 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Robots Legal Boundary model · Theory Robots Lega…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A robots allow rule is complete legal permission to collect and reuse content.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-robots-legal-boundary-lojdvd(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_robots_legal_boundary_lojdvd\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?charterCases < 10)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?provenCases < 9 \|\| provenCases > charterCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?revocationCases < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?authorityGaps > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return

checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
